[Pitch in $5 now to support our winning effort >>](https://bayh.bsd.net/page/m/32a504b5/7be36b49/1e892327/78f86a9f/2227317810/VEsE/ "https://secure.actblue.com/contribute/page/bayh-email?refcode=20160919.2nd&amount=10")**

* _**THEN** :_ They also reported that "outside groups have picked up the slack with a string of anti-Bayh attacks."
* _**NOW** :_ _CNN_ is reporting that the Koch brothers' political groups are undertaking a HUGE field campaign in Indiana.

The next 50 days mean everything. | Derek --

I’ve run my last campaign.

But the things that I’ve fought for my entire career -- tolerance, democracy, justice, the progress we’ve made -- they’re all on the ballot in 50 days.

I’ll see it as a personal insult to my legacy and the work we’ve done together if we fail to step up and make sure that Hillary takes my place in January.

**So please, chip in to make sure Hillary’s team can get the folks on the ground that they need to turn out the voters who will win this thing -- and help them reach their goal of 2.5 million donors by the end of the month. They’ll send you a free sticker to say thank you:** [](https://links.hillaryclinton.com/ctt?kn=7&ms=MjY1NTM1MjYS1&r=MTA3MzIzMDg3NjIwS0&b=0&j=ODgzMDQ4MzA2S0&mt=1&rt=0)
Thank you,

Barack

|

subject: Which issues do you care about most?
disclaimer_text: Paid for by Trump Make America Great Again Committee, a joint fundraising committee authorized by and composed of Donald J
body:
We need your input immediately. ||
* * *
Please find a special message from one of our advertisers, Trump Make America Great Again Committee.
* * *
---
[](http://email.bencarson.com/ct/37655847:sxum2HbNJ:m:1:876315805:7862E25327EC5A8729236EBECE7DD41F:r)

Peter,

Kellyanne, my campaign manager, asked you to complete our Platform for America Survey.

So many Americans have already written in, giving their honest feedback on how to Make America Great Again!

But I want to hear from you, too, Peter.

[**Please complete the Platform for America survey today.**](http://email.bencarson.com/ct/37655847:sxum2HbNJ:m:1:876315805:7862E25327EC5A8729236EBECE7DD41F:r)

You'll have a hand in crafting the message we take to all 50 states.

And it's already working. Our Platform for America is resonating across the country.

We're up 7 nationally, we're up 5 in Ohio, we're up 4 in Florida, we're up 2 in Nevada, and we're up 4 in Colorado.

I know you receive a lot of surveys from us. But if you need any proof that these surveys work, look at our success in the polls. It's thanks to grassroots leaders like you weighing in on important issues that we're able to focus on what the American people truly care about.

Now take a stand and give your input on the most pressing issues our country faces.

[**Complete the Platform for America Survey as soon as you can.**](http://email.bencarson.com/ct/37655847:sxum2HbNJ:m:1:876315805:7862E25327EC5A8729236EBECE7DD41F:r)

Thanks,

Donald J. Trump

body:

Friend, I’m looking for one supporter to travel with me on the Trump campaign plane to a rally. Are you interested? Just contribute $3 to be automatically entered for your chance to win. It’s on the campaign plane that we as a team discuss our strategy, review our speeches, and plot our next move on the campaign trail. So while we’re up in the air, you’ll have the opportunity to share your thoughts on our campaign, and your vision for our country’s future. And when we get to the rally, you’ll get your picture taken with me so that you can remember this incredible day for the rest of your life. Plus your generous contribution of $3 helps our campaign win BIG this November. It’s a WIN-WIN, Friend. Contribute $3 to be automatically entered to win. Good luck! Donald J. name: Luke Hilgemann
subject: Stop lame-duck spending
body:
Heads up Peter- this is a long email, but we want you to fully understand the implications of this strategic fight
---
| |
---
| | | |

---
| | | |

---
| | [ ](http://click.myprosperitymail.com/?qs=813e0035803925d6d86953cd8df1c85aa36e65499aa358e7f4d363e9f54f2a30ea0e1b30f87cc393 "https://americansforprosperity.org/")
---
| | Heads up Peter- this is a long email, but we want you to fully understand the implications of this strategic fight...Congress faced a test this month: they determined how to fund the government beyond the end of the fiscal year. How they handled this revealed that too many lawmakers are more interested in campaigning back home than addressing our nation's serious fiscal problems. Disappointingly, lawmakers are poised to consider legislation that only funds the government through the middle of lame duck session, when they'll have to consider another spending bill. This is a dangerous time for supporters of limited government like you and me.Here's two reasons the lame-duck option is bad for you: At least 44 members of Congress will be retired or fired and many of them will be hired as lobbyists. They'll be tempted during the lame-duck session to throw one last bone to their future K Street colleagues before they leave the legislature. **The last thing taxpayers deserve is another lame-duck Congress where unaccountable lawmakers ratchet-up spending and cement pet projects for corporate special interests. ** There's another strategic element to this. Without getting too into the weeds, there are tax provisions and spending items that are set to expire. If they expire it will **lay the groundwork for comprehensive, long-term tax reform**. If they are extended during a lame-duck session, then it makes the prospect of comprehensive reform very unlikely in the near future. In other words: this is our chance. But as you can guess, many in Congress are thinking more about votes in November than they are about fiscal responsibility in 2017 and beyond. That's why we are leading the charge to **make them care about this issue** . We've seen this fight coming and have been preparing for this battle for much of the year. A trillion in spending should be negotiated by elected officials who will answer to voters.
We're also working in Washington to make sure the Congressmen and their staffs are clear about the implications of allowing a spending package to come up in the lame-duck session. This has been a monumental effort and I'm proud of the work the AFP team has done--with your help--to hold lawmakers accountable and create real change in Washington. But we need the resources to be able to fund the fight. This is a strategic initiative that will have ripple effects for years to come! If there's any way possible, please **[give $25 or $50](http://click.myprosperitymail.com/?qs=813e0035803925d68f76edc11252b0a61d62d72026c74897f87083e5e7901e31ffe95e0f833f1c2a "give $25 or $50")** to help fund the work that AFP is coordinating around the country and in Washington. Our field staff is working relentlessly to make sure your tax dollars aren't used for wasteful spending. Please continue to **[support AFP](http://click.myprosperitymail.com/?qs=813e0035803925d6cd972d1aa4d9bfe0d30740754a42a07f7a833510510ad1475a68b5b936201258 "support AFP")** so we can continue to hold our lawmakers accountable to do what's best for the people they serve. Will you [**support our strategic battle today****?**](http://click.myprosperitymail.com/?qs=813e0035803925d6d0da1e316d077a03bec80d82b294a9235e76f27abb15fceddee2841369ff455c "support our strategic battle today?") Thanks,-LukeLuke Hilgemann
CEO, Americans for Prosperity
---

name: Mike Weinholtz
subject: Mike Weinholtz for Governor Newsletter
disclaimer_text: Paid for by Mike Weinholtz for Governor
body:
| | [](https://act.myngp.com/el/-7142843143270757632/2119173415863782144)
---
**_MIKE WEINHOLTZ FOR GOVERNOR NEWSLETTER_** _Haz click_[ _aqu i_](https://act.myngp.com/el/-7142843143270757632/2191231009901710080) _para leerlo en espa ñol!_ **_MESSAGE FROM MIKE_** Last Friday, the Utah League of Cities and Towns hosted the first gubernatorial debate between Gov. Herbert and myself. He'd originally declined, but after pressure from the League, my campaign, and an online petition with thousands of signatures, he agreed to the debate. (Special thanks to all of you who signed the petition!) [**Because of his late RSVP, the press didn 't cover the full debate but you can watch it here:**](https://act.myngp.com/el/-7142843143270757632/2263288603939638016) [](https://act.myngp.com/el/-7142843143270757632/2335346197977565952) **_Why is it so hard to get Gov. Herbert just to participate in the democratic process?_** The sitting governor of Utah has participated in the ULCT debate every year since 1988, but it wasn't until after pressure from multiple sources that Gov. Herbert reversed course and agreed to it. He blamed his evasion on scheduling, saying that he couldn't "fit it in" because of his black-tie fundraising gala that night, even though the debate was midday. (By the way, it's expected that Gov. "Available Jones" made an easy $1 million dollars just in that ONE evening, thanks to corporate donors in attendance like fossil-fuel giant Anadarko Petroleum Co.) | I'm so tired of this. Gary Herbert is our governor, the highest elected office in the state, yet he _consistently_ puts fundraising ahead of governing, puts corporate interests ahead of everyday Utahns, and puts politics ahead of people.
---
Enough is enough!
There are 7 weeks left until Election Day on November 8th. Together let's send a clear message that Utahns want big money OUT of our state politics, and that we don't want a third term from "Available Jones!"
**Click**[**HERE**](https://act.myngp.com/el/-7142843143270757632/2407403792015493888)**to register to vote,**[**HERE**](https://act.myngp.com/el/-7142843143270757632/2479461386053421824)**to volunteer, and**[**HERE**](https://act.myngp.com/el/-7142843143270757632/2551518980091349760)**to donate!**
Together with you!
Mike
* * *
**_" THIS LAND IS THEIR LAND"_**
The always-amazing Pat Bagley gave his take on the gubernatorial debate. It's too good not to share:
* * *
**_THE AMERICAN DREAM_**
I've been blessed to be a walking example of the American Dream. I'm running for governor because I want to make sure that EVERY Utahn has that same opportunity.
**[Check out our newest campaign video,"The American Dream":](https://act.myngp.com/el/-7142843143270757632/2623576574129277696?v=fQMfnnBXn80)**
[](https://act.myngp.com/el/-7142843143270757632/2695634168167205632?v=fQMfnnBXn80)
* * *
**_MORE PROTECTING, LESS PROFITEERING_**
While Gov. Herbert was asking corporations for cash at his black-tie gala, I spent the evening talking to concerned voters at a town hall about keeping Utah clean and beautiful. It's always inspiring to see so many individuals committed to protecting Utah from those who would destroy it through greed and negligence. (Special thanks to the Environmental Ministry and the First Unitarian Church of Salt Lake for hosting this great event!)

After 6 years of the Herbert administration, we have some of the worst air in the nation. We have crumbling water infrastructure and toxic algae blooms in our Utah lakes. To top it all off, rather than investing resources to fix those problems, instead Gov. Herbert signed the Transfer of Public Lands Act and launched us into a $14 million dollar lawsuit to sue the federal government over it.
Our elected officials should be working to PROTECT Utah-- not PROFIT off of it.
**[Click here to sign the petition and tell Gov. Herbert that Utahns want our home protected, not sold to the highest bidder!](https://act.myngp.com/el/-7142843143270757632/2767691762205133568)**
[](https://act.myngp.com/el/-7142843143270757632/2839749356243061504)
* * *
**_PROVO PRIDE_**
I've been a staunch supporter of the LGBTQ community here in Utah for years. I helped to start an LGBTQ pride group at my former company, CHG Healthcare. I'm on the board of the ACLU of Utah, and my wife is on the board of Equality Utah because we believe that equality for ALL Utahns is important but also know that won't happen on its own.
That's why I was so excited to attend Provo Pride 2016! It's reaffirming and inspiring to see Utahns statewide turning out to show support for the values we all share: equality, respect, and kindness!
* * *
**_My campaign isn 't taking any corporate money, which means that the only way we can win is through support from individuals like you!_**
**_Click_**[** _here_**](https://act.myngp.com/el/-7142843143270757632/2551518980091349760)** _to contribute now!_**

Paid for by Mike Weinholtz for Governor
---

Mike for Utah
193 W. 2100 S.
South Salt Lake UT 84115 United States

name: Tom Russell, RussFeingold.com
subject: He's set for life
disclaimer_text: paid for by Russ for Wisconsin, Joe Sensenbrenner, Madison, WI, Treasurer
body:
Senator Ron Johnson does not have to worry about retirement. He is set for life. | [](http://action.russfeingold.com/page/m/66cf455f/1c469400/9630dd/3579ada/2447614395/VEsH/ "http://salsa.wiredforchange.com/o/6405/p/dia/action3/common/public/?action_KEY=12472") Friend, Senator Ron Johnson doesn't worry about whether or not he'll be able to retire with dignity. He paid himself a $10 million corporate golden parachute. _He's set for life._ **Senator Johnson and his billionaire friends don't rely on Social Security, which is why he ridicules it as a "Ponzi scheme" and wants it privatized. His family's retirement isn't on the line.** He doesn't understand that Social Security is a promise we made to American seniors to help ensure they don't live in poverty after they stop working. Over one million Wisconsinites depend on Social Security, with many more preparing for a retirement that relies on it. Imagine what'd happen to them and their families if Senator Johnson had his way. **[Stand with Russ and our friends at Social Security Works. Add your name if you agree: We need to expand, not cut, Social Security.](http://action.russfeingold.com/page/m/66cf455f/1c469400/9630dd/3579ada/2447614395/VEsE/ "http://salsa.wiredforchange.com/o/6405/p/dia/action3/common/public/?action_KEY=12472")** Millions of Americans invest to help fund their retirements. But when the Great Recession hit, wages stagnated and Wall Street greed destroyed countless retirement dreams. But folks looking to retire knew that Social Security was still there for them. But right-wing extremists like Senator Johnson desperately want to rip our seniors' retirement security of their hands, and offer it up to reckless Wall Street billionaires. **Seniors shouldn't be at the mercy of Wall Street for their retirement.** Instead of cutting Social Security, we should be strengthening it, expanding benefits for millions of Americans and ensuring a secure retirement for all. **[Stand with Russ and Social Security Works today: Tell the Senate exactly where you stand on Social Security.](http://action.russfeingold.com/page/m/66cf455f/1c469400/9630dd/3579ada/2447614395/VEsF/ "http://salsa.wiredforchange.com/o/6405/p/dia/action3/common/public/?action_KEY=12472")** Thanks, Tom Russell

Friend -- Playing the President in _The West Wing_ teaches you to honor the best of our politics, not the worst of it.

Constitutional duty, bipartisanship, respect: We all hold dear these bedrock values of our democracy, no matter our differences.

So when Senate Republicans' extreme partisanship puts GOP priorities above the will of the people, there's a powerful mandate we must deliver in an election year.

[**That 's why I'm proud as a Democrat (and on behalf of President Bartlet) to launch the DSCC's 48-hour Race to the Majority campaign. Friend, will you join us and pitch in today to seize our best chance in years to take back the Senate?**](https://secure.dscc. **Democrats can change that instantly with a 2016 Senate map in our favor: We need to pick up just FOUR Republican seats to demote Mitch McConnell and restore Congress.**

disclaimer_text: Paid for by the Evan Bayh Committee, 133 W
body:
| [ ](https://bayh.bsd.net/page/m/32a504b2/7be36b49/1e977995/78f86931/3494264012/VEsH/ "http://www.evanbayhforindiana.com/")
---
Friend,

Last week, one of Congressman Young's Washington allies posted a disgusting tweet that attacked Evan's sons, Beau and Nick.

Attacks on family members should have no place in this or any political contest. But these are the kinds of dirty tactics we're fighting as we work to elect a senator who truly embodies Indiana values.**[Friend, will you help us take on the shameful attacks and fight even harder to elect Evan by pitching in $5 to our campaign today?](https://bayh.bsd.net/page/m/32a504b2/7be36b49/1e977995/78f86930/3494264012/VEsE/ "https://secure.actblue.com/contribute/page/bayh-email?refcode=20160920nd&amount=10")**

I've known Evan and his family for three decades. > | | | | **TO:** Interested Parties **FROM:** Conrad Lucas, Chairman, West Virginia Republican Party **RE:** Recent Polling Data **DATE:** September 19, 2016 With the campaign season now in full swing, the West Virginia Republican Party recently commissioned a statewide poll of likely voters to gauge the temperature of the electorate. Our focus in the poll was on statewide races and various issues of importance to West Virginians. The WVGOP worked with _Just Win Strategies_ \- a national political consulting and research firm based in Washington, DC. The WVGOP has worked with the team from _Just Win Strategies_ for multiple election cycles.
>
> The statewide survey was stratified by Designated Market Area (DMA) and current voter registration statistics. Current voter registration statistics in West Virginia indicate approximately 46% of voters are registered Democrats, approximately 30% are registered Republicans and approximately 24% are either Independent, Unaffiliated with a political party or registered in another party.
>
> The survey sample size was 600 with a margin of error of +/- 4% and was conducted on September 8th and 10th.
>
> Of key interest is the question of which political party voters in West Virginia identify with most. While registration trends have seen the Democrat Party on a downward spiral with Republicans and Unaffiliated slowly gaining, this is the first data commissioned statewide by the WVGOP that showcases the clear party preference turn to Republican. When asked, "Which Party Do You Identify With?" the response was as follows:
>
> **Republican** \- 37% **Democrat** \- 32% **Independent/Other** \- 31%
>
> Clearly, the last eight years of the Democrat Party's war on coal and sharp turn to the left has harmed the Party's image with its own voters.
>
> The preference for President of the United States was reflective of this strong change to Republican. When asked about the presidential race, the results were as follows:
>
> **Donald Trump** \- 57% **Hillary Clinton** \- 30% **Gary Johnson** \- 4% **Undecided** \- 9% Following Mitt Romney's fifty-five county sweep of West Virginia in 2012 and Hillary Clinton's loss in the Democrat primary in the Mountain State, the results thus far come as no surprise. Regarding statewide races, the most heated race with the most attention is that of Governor. The polling results show a tightening race:
>
> **Democrat Jim Justice** \- 44% **Republican Bill Cole** \- 42% **Mountain Party Charlotte Pritt** \- 3% **Constitution Party Phil Hudock** \- 2% **Libertarian Party David Moran** \- 2% **Undecided** \- 7% The race for governor is within the margin of error and can be considered a tie at this point.
>
> As with all polls and with all elections, the key to winning is turnout. In the coming weeks, it is essential for all Republicans and conservatives to rally around our nominees for statewide offices in order to ensure the change our state deserve takes place.
>
> From the data, it is clear that a large portion of Democrats no longer want to support their own Party for very clear reasons. Our slate of statewide candidates with conservative Bill Cole at the top will begin to set a course of success for our state - but they have to win first.
>
>

clean_body: | | | Friend -- There's a new poll out and it has Jim Justice and Hillary Clinton scared. It confirms what we've been saying all along - this is going to be a fight until the finish. This race is in a dead heat. You can see the statement and polling numbers from Chairman Conrad Lucas below. We have just 5 days until our fundraising reporting deadline and we need your help to make sure we have the resources to fight until the end. can defeat the corruption and good ol' boy network that has held our state back for so long. But we can't do it without your help. Every dollar helps! \- we can and we will win this fight. Thank you for your support. Your friend, Rachel Wilson Finance Director P.S. With only 5 days left, we need your help. You can donate online right now through this Or you can mail in a contribution to P.O. Box 11481, Charleston, WV 25339. Thanks again. | | | | TO: Interested Parties FROM: Conrad Lucas, Chairman, West Virginia Republican Party RE: Recent Polling Data DATE: September 19, 2016 With the campaign season now in full swing, the West Virginia Republican Party recently commissioned a statewide poll of likely voters to gauge the temperature of the electorate. Our focus in the poll was on statewide races and various issues of importance to West Virginians. The WVGOP worked with Just Win Strategies \- a national political consulting and research firm based in Washington, DC. The WVGOP has worked with the team from Just Win Strategies for multiple election cycles. The statewide survey was stratified by Designated Market Area (DMA) and current voter registration statistics. Current voter registration statistics in West Virginia indicate approximately 46% of voters are registered Democrats, approximately 30% are registered Republicans and approximately 24% are either Independent, Unaffiliated with a political party or registered in another party. The survey sample size was 600 with a margin of error of +/- 4% and was conducted on September 8th and 10th. Of key interest is the question of which political party voters in West Virginia identify with most. While registration trends have seen the Democrat Party on a downward spiral with Republicans and Unaffiliated slowly gaining, this is the first data commissioned statewide by the WVGOP that showcases the clear party preference turn to Republican. When asked, "Which Party Do You Identify With?" the response was as follows: Republican \- 37% Democrat \- 32% Independent/Other \- 31% Clearly, the last eight years of the Democrat Party's war on coal and sharp turn to the left has harmed the Party's image with its own voters. The preference for President of the United States was reflective of this strong change to Republican. When asked about the presidential race, the results were as follows: Donald Trump \- 57% Hillary Clinton \- 30% Gary Johnson \- 4% Undecided \- 9% Following Mitt Romney's fifty-five county sweep of West Virginia in 2012 and Hillary Clinton's loss in the Democrat primary in the Mountain State, the results thus far come as no surprise. Regarding statewide races, the most heated race with the most attention is that of Governor. The polling results show a tightening race: Democrat Jim Justice \- 44% Republican Bill Cole \- 42% Mountain Party Charlotte Pritt \- 3% Constitution Party Phil Hudock \- 2% Libertarian Party David Moran \- 2% Undecided \- 7% The race for governor is within the margin of error and can be considered a tie at this point. As with all polls and with all elections, the key to winning is turnout. In the coming weeks, it is essential for all Republicans and conservatives to rally around our nominees for statewide offices in order to ensure the change our state deserve takes place. From the data, it is clear that a large portion of Democrats no longer want to support their own Party for very clear reasons. Our slate of statewide candidates with conservative Bill Cole at the top will begin to set a course of success for our state - but they have to win first. ** ###** Paid for by the WV Republican Party, PO Box 2711, Charleston, WV 25301 Not authorized by any candidate or their committee. --- | | | | | | | | | ---|---|---|---|--- | PAID FOR BY FRIENDS OF COLE Friends of Bill Cole · United States

Peter,

Momentum is on our side, but I need your help. Today I was endorsed by the Missouri Corrections Officers Association. That follows our endorsement by the statewide Chamber of Commerce.

We need your help to keep the momentum going. If we can get our message to the people of Missouri, I know that they will recognize that our state is ready for positive change from outside of the political class.

Now the attacks are coming. My opponent, career-politician Chris Koster launched a new attack ad today. name: Hillary Clinton
subject: Here’s my promise for the debate:
disclaimer_text: Paid for by Hillary for America, a grassroots campaign of 2
body:
I'm ready to give my all | Derek --

For more than a year now, Donald Trump has used his platform on the campaign trail to try to silence and bully anyone who looks or acts differently than him.

So here’s what I’m promising you today: When I step onto the debate stage a week from now, I’m going to use _my_ voice, my platform, and my microphone to speak out on behalf of the Americans he’s trying to put down.

I’m going to stand up for the 11-year-old girl in Nevada, who’s worried that her parents would be deported under a Trump presidency. For the Muslim family in Queens whose young children are terrified of how their family would be treated if Donald Trump becomes their Commander-in-Chief. I’ll speak up for every American who needs someone in their corner -- not someone backing them into one.

Derek, I’ve taken part in quite a few debates in my life. But Monday’s is the most important. With so much on the line, I’m ready to give my all.

**It’s going to be a historic night, and I’d love for you to be there for it. Will you add your name before tonight’s final deadline for a shot to sit in the audience as I challenge Trump? Time’s running out, the contest ends at midnight:** [](https://links.hillaryclinton.com/ctt?kn=16&ms=MjY1NjA0NDkS1&r=MTA3MzIzMDg3NjIwS0&b=0&j=ODgzMTczNjUyS0&mt=1&rt=0)
I hope to see you there!

Hillary

name: OurRevolution.com
subject: 10 local candidates who need our help
disclaimer_text: PAID FOR BY OUR REVOLUTION
body:
#
Sisters and Brothers,
Change never happens from the top down, it always occurs from the bottom up. This is just as true for how we can win long-lasting progressive change in our cities and our states.
We want to help progressives who are fighting for change at the local level. **So today we're endorsing 10 local candidates who represent the future of our political revolution.**
Some of these candidates could help determine how congressional districts are drawn for the remainder of the next decade -- a key to progressives gaining power in the House of Representatives for the decade to come. Others can use their offices to create real change for voting rights, equal pay, and economic justice.
It's up to us to transform the future of our country, and that's why we are proud to ask:
[**Can we count on you to split a $2.70 contribution to this great slate of progressive local candidates and Our Revolution as a way of saying we are going to fight to transform our country from the bottom up?**](https://secure.actblue.com/contribute/page/10-local-candidates?refcode=em160920-full-nd "https://secure.actblue.com/contribute/page/10-local-candidates?refcode=em160920-full-nd")
**Sara Niccoli is a farmer and advocate for working people running for New York's state senate.** She's running on a platform that will always put her community ahead of corporate interests. A victory for Sara would help turn the tide against the narrow majority in New York's senate held by conservatives and corporate-backed politicians.
**Teresa Meyer is a registered nurse who would be a tremendous state representative in Iowa.** She wants to increase the minimum wage, and she's seen in her career how difficult it can be for patients to navigate health insurance companies to get the care they need. She'll be on the side of working families in Iowa.
**Brad Avakian has spent his career fighting for working people, and now he's running to be Oregon's next Secretary of State.** As the state's labor commissioner, Brad fought wage theft and civil rights abuses. And if Brad wins, he plans to use his office to ensure Oregon corporations provide equal pay for equal work.
**Adrian Fontes should be Maricopa County, Arizona's next County Recorder.** You might remember huge lines, delays, and disenfranchisement during Arizona's presidential primary. Well, that was partially thanks to Adrian's opponent, because the county recorder is in charge of voting access and rights in Arizona's biggest county. Adrian is running to restore voting rights and to protect democracy.
**Dan Quick is a proud union member running to be a state senator in Nebraska.** Dan's fought against corporate privatization of public utilities, and he would be a strong advocate for working families in Nebraska's state senate.
**Mari Cordes is a registered nurse running for Vermont's state house who knows we need universal health care.** She wants to remove corporate greed from our health care system because she's seen how insurance companies get in the way of patient care. That's why Mari will fight for a publicly-funded health care system.
**Gary Kroeger is running as a proud progressive to be a state representative in Iowa.** He will fight to protect students, the environment, and working people, and will help advance the ideals of our political revolution.
**Jane Kim is a civil rights attorney who's fought for affordable housing and fair wages and who is running for California's state senate.** You've met her before: Bernie Sanders' endorsement, along with thousands of contributions from our political revolution, helped Jane pull off a shocking win in her primary in June. Now she needs our help in her general election to secure another huge victory.
**Nicole Cannizzaro knows the value of public education, and that's why she's running to be a state senator in Nevada.** She benefited from Nevada's state-funded educational opportunities that helped her go to college and become a lawyer. She'll fight to protect and expand those programs in Nevada's state senate.
**Sabrina Shrader uses her experience growing up in poverty to help others, and now she's running for West Virginia's state house.** After a difficult childhood, Sabrina shares her story with other families struggling in poverty and works to help them get back on their feet. She would be a tremendous advocate in West Virginia's legislature.
[**Can you split a $2.70 contribution to these candidates and Our Revolution as a way of saying we are going to fight to transform our country from top to bottom?**](https://secure.actblue.com/contribute/page/10-local-candidates?refcode=em160920-full-nd "https://secure.actblue.com/contribute/page/10-local-candidates?refcode=em160920-full-nd")
We have the opportunity to help these folks win their local races. If they're successful, they'll help create lasting progressive policies, and some might even go on to be members of Congress, senators, and governors.
That is the political revolution, and that's why splitting your contribution between this great slate of local candidates and Our Revolution is so important.
In solidarity,
Shannon Jackson
Executive Director
Our Revolution
**[CONTRIBUTE](https://secure.actblue.com/contribute/page/10-local-candidates?refcode=em160920-full-nd "https://secure.actblue.com/contribute/page/10-local-candidates?refcode=em160920-full-nd")**

PAID FOR BY OUR REVOLUTION

NOT AUTHORIZED BY ANY CANDIDATE OR CANDIDATE COMMITTEE.

name: Eric Holcomb
subject: Taking Indiana to the next level
disclaimer_text: Paid for by Eric Holcomb for Indiana
body:
In this election, we have the chance to continue Indiana’s momentum and take our state to the next level. | | | | | [ ](http://links.holcombforindiana.com/u/7728507d56ea4faea21f5b8186dfded2?_e=dpwillis67%40gmail.com&_m=818cd858a1384cb8be2e0b4d8b9613e3&utm_source=Iterable&utm_medium=Image_Header_Logo&utm_term=Economy&utm_campaign=GFR&utm_content=20160920-taking-indiana-to-the-next-level-16266-petition)
---
| | | | Friend, In this election, we have the chance to continue Indiana's momentum and take our state to the next level. _I 'm running for governor to do just that._ Today, we introduced the first part of our _**Taking Indiana to the Next Level**_ plan, which outlines the specific policies Suzanne and I intend to pursue if honored to serve as your governor and lieutenant governor. **First and foremost, we 'll focus on economic development, which will strengthen and diversify our thriving Hoosier jobs climate.** To start, we will maximize our efforts to **retain, retrain, and recruit the best and the brightest.** That is why our plan calls for a $1 billion investment in entrepreneurship and innovation over the next ten years coupled with expanding entrepreneur programs in high schools as well as increasing the number of grants offered to start-ups. We also understand the need to **lead the nation in infrastructure, investment and innovation.** We'll use data-driven solutions, not political considerations, to continue our record-setting investments in our state's roads, bridges, airports and ports. We'll also do everything in our power to **keep Hoosier energy costs down.** We're going to start by opposing burdensome EPA regulations while developing an all-of-the-above Indiana approach when it comes to utilizing our state's own abundant and reliable energy resources. And last, but not least, we're going to champion fiscal discipline by passing another honestly balanced budget and maintaining our healthy rainy day fund to preserve our AAA credit rating. This economic development plan will help us continue to build and grow Indiana - and take us to the Next Level!
**[Join us by pledging your support in strengthening our economic development.](http://links.holcombforindiana.com/u/44f2082ad24046338168facb43b7da92?_e=dpwillis67%40gmail.com&_m=818cd858a1384cb8be2e0b4d8b9613e3&utm_source=Iterable&utm_medium=text_body_cta&utm_term=Economy&utm_campaign=GFR&utm_content=20160920-taking-indiana-to-the-next-level-16266-petition)**
---
| | [ **Add my name** ](http://links.holcombforindiana.com/u/24e079e9f1f94b7898f572fc932becdf?_e=dpwillis67%40gmail.com&_m=818cd858a1384cb8be2e0b4d8b9613e3&utm_source=Iterable&utm_medium=button_body_text&utm_term=Economy&utm_campaign=GFR&utm_content=20160920-taking-indiana-to-the-next-level-16266-petition)
---
Full steam ahead!Eric Holcomb

| | Hi there,

If you haven't heard, this could be our best shot to flip the Senate for years.

There's a lot of good news on this front: We have six women running to flip seats, and we need to flip just five. We have a strong candidate -- Catherine Cortez Masto -- running to keep Harry Reid's critical Nevada Senate seat blue. And thanks to Trump and our GOP opponents, a number of these races have been re-rated in favor of Democrats.

Everyone's saying it: Women are the key to flipping the Senate this year. And you know what that means? EMILY's List is key, too.

**[Donate $3 today to help flip the Senate with pro-choice Democratic women.](https://secure.emilyslist.org/page/m/118c0ec6/24c69fd9/1933bf88/18e92df5/3076780628/VEsE/ "https://secure.emilyslist.org/page/contribute/20160920_email_MoreWomen?utm_medium=email&utm_source=emilyslist&utm_content=2+-+Donate+3+today+to+help+flip+the+Senate+w&utm_campaign=WPEFEU39N_digital_email_20160920_SenateGrow_n-all&source=WPEFEU39N_digital_email_20160920_SenateGrow_n-all")**

_Rothenberg & Gonzales Political Report_ names New Hampshire, Nevada, Pennsylvania, and North Carolina among their tightest races. That's right -- EMILY's List women are at the center of four of Democrats' best chances to flip the Senate and keep critical seats blue.

If all of our Senate red-to-blue seats flip and Catherine wins, women will be 40 percent of the Democratic caucus next year. We're going to keep going and get to 50 percent -- and we'll keep going after that to make up for lost time!

But first we have some big races we have to win, and right now they're all close.

**Donate $3 to make sure our women flip the Senate:**

**[https://secure.emilyslist.org/Grow-Womens-Numbers](https://secure.emilyslist.org/page/m/118c0ec6/24c69fd9/1933bf88/18e92df6/3076780628/VEsF/ "https://secure.emilyslist.org/page/contribute/20160920_email_MoreWomen?utm_medium=email&utm_source=emilyslist&utm_content=3+-+httpssecureemilyslistorgGrowWomensNumber&utm_campaign=WPEFEU39N_digital_email_20160920_SenateGrow_n-all&source=WPEFEU39N_digital_email_20160920_SenateGrow_n-all")**

body:
This is the fourth announcement of the NHGOP State Convention to
be held on Saturday, September 24th at Saint Anselm College.

The delegate list has been posted at www.nh.gop/convention ( http://email.robly.com/wf/click?upn=gT2AZ1ZnBy-2FrecDREa9-2BbUwdCsIkt2CtnDJdhyJ3atmvNzzJtNbk6oiOy3Rl7fDL_enNSH-2FxHAAqXgxiHRFihpX91d75ERAkpfg4XQfmYhCFb47pW8iDRAYLsM9sf9wIa92fG-2FsxU68zXzzMA2nO2VCunl2bm-2FhLXsWS8P3XITs28GUU7Mnlu37POPaWv9myv4YWBbFU9plZi0-2B42tZfBaUujH4mxA3e6jOiyDa5q6HKz45-2B17g6DUNL-2BnIMAR30M7I3-2B3Rlu5GJF2A-2F3eI3sFfHDIu54Bkdcor-2FHMJEER3c2EW5OY8OkDq0hZm4yzifcn2-2B48ZO5v72mwWLaPvXKUgJYFJlBa-2FGjdJeGN5hcxzZL73rBEP7cJk1ayI1Fgs7yz9qh1eLrCEwYhV3fjWzCKVYMnI9-2BkQ9qWtXjxcxMlJ0FHssY8h3mCxGae75jzBoapUulG5Pxu3NAUAw-2Bnj3vgiOYLa1RCj0uV0pbi81n4HoR9V1vZAGlHbgwIWp67UhT ). The only list available currently
is for those who ran solely for the position of delegate. Those
who will serve as delegates as a result of winning a Republican
Primary will be posted when the list become available.

A postcard will be mailed to all the delegates, postmarked
tomorrow.

The event will be held at the Dana Center on the campus of Saint
Anselm College located at 100 St Anselm Dr, Manchester, NH 03102.
Signs will be posted to facilitate parking and walking to the
venue from the lots.

The convention will gavel at 9:00am sharp and will run no later
than 12:30pm. Registration will begin at 7:45am.

The platform committee has met three times and is still accepting
applications for amendments.

Anyone wishing to submit a platform amendment to be heard by the
platform committee must submit their proposal using this form ( http://email.robly.com/wf/click?upn=3qP3xl6Iy86fMdiFlFopyH8ubnW4EyLnMtnx9XO20Tf6j15RcZzUgHzaJbmryesE_enNSH-2FxHAAqXgxiHRFihpX91d75ERAkpfg4XQfmYhCFb47pW8iDRAYLsM9sf9wIa92fG-2FsxU68zXzzMA2nO2VCunl2bm-2FhLXsWS8P3XITs28GUU7Mnlu37POPaWv9myv4YWBbFU9plZi0-2B42tZfBaUujH4mxA3e6jOiyDa5q6HKz45-2B17g6DUNL-2BnIMAR30M7I3-2B3Rlu5GJF2A-2F3eI3sFfHDIu54Bkdcor-2FHMJEER3c2EW5OY8OkDq0hZm4yzifcn2-2B48ZO5v72mwWLaPvXKUoNCC1SqW8-2FfABwosWUT05v-2Fw3h5yx6zi2OOrXZVhIZ71d28z4BlfDM32YD4otmLnxb7-2FXGRZFJfMfPveJQCrKamV9z-2BgcpJSlg7F2MBOc4-2FDdm9ul-2Ff-2FG9hUbaBCVdFPmseAljA6skwAvsr5M1It7wnbbBbRkACqEbY5FjRJ53i ) by 5:00pm, Wednesday

clean_body: This is the fourth announcement of the NHGOP State Convention to be held on Saturday, September 24th at Saint Anselm College. The delegate list has been posted at www.nh.gop/convention ( ). The only list available currently is for those who ran solely for the position of delegate. Those who will serve as delegates as a result of winning a Republican Primary will be posted when the list become available. A postcard will be mailed to all the delegates, postmarked tomorrow. The event will be held at the Dana Center on the campus of Saint Anselm College located at 100 St Anselm Dr, Manchester, NH 03102. Signs will be posted to facilitate parking and walking to the venue from the lots. The convention will gavel at 9:00am sharp and will run no later than 12:30pm. Registration will begin at 7:45am. The platform committee has met three times and is still accepting applications for amendments. Anyone wishing to submit a platform amendment to be heard by the platform committee must submit their proposal using this form ( ) by 5:00pm, Wednesday September 21st. As always, please continue to check www.nh.gop/convention ( ) for further updates. Paid for by the New Hampshire Republican State Committee Not authorized by any candidate or candidate's committee. www.nh.gop footer image

Peter,

Eric Greitens will be hosting an event to honor three American heroes next Tuesday.

Congressional Medal of Honor recipients Michael Thornton and Thomas Norris, along with renowned military author and Navy SEAL Dick Couch, are coming to show their support of Eric next Tuesday, September 27th, at an event to honor those heroes in St. Louis.
[](http://ericgreitens.us13.list-manage.com/track/click?u=c81357bdb59fb170d9eb82b3a&id=777d1ce88d&e=84041254e6)
Don't miss this chance to meet these incredible men who risked their lives for their country. _[Register for the event here](http://ericgreitens.us13.list-manage1.com/track/click?u=c81357bdb59fb170d9eb82b3a&id=1ecb215795&e=84041254e6)._

The event will be at VFW post 3944 at 3:30 pm. The address is 10817 Midland Blvd, Overland, MO 63114.

-Team Greitens
